data_flow_trace.original: Pool voter calls Governance.createProposal(stakePool, targets, datas, uri) at Governance.sol:253 — sets expirationTime, executionHash; isResolved=false, resolutionTime=0.
data_flow_trace.current_state_summary: createProposal still sets expirationTime=now+votingDuration, stores executionHash=keccak256(abi.encode(targets,datas)), and initializes isResolved=false/resolutionTime=0 (lines 322-334). Behavior matches description.
data_flow_trace.gating_conditions: Caller must be pool's voter (_requireVoter line 297); pool must be valid; votingPower >= requiredProposerStake; targets.length in [1, MAX_PROPOSAL_TARGETS].
data_flow_trace.differs_from_original: Line reference shifted (was 253, now 276-337) but semantics identical.

data_flow_trace.original: Voters call vote()/_voteInternal at Governance.sol:352 — accumulates p.yesVotes/p.noVotes; lastVoteTime[id] written. _voteInternal requires now_ < p.expirationTime.
data_flow_trace.current_state_summary: _voteInternal reverts if now_>=p.expirationTime (line 396-398); accumulates yesVotes/noVotes (lines 429-433); records lastVoteTime[proposalId] (line 437). Matches description.
data_flow_trace.gating_conditions: Proposal exists, voting window open, not resolved, pool valid, caller is voter.
data_flow_trace.differs_from_original: Only line numbers shifted; logic unchanged.

data_flow_trace.original: Time advances past expirationTime. NO ONE calls resolve(); p.isResolved stays false, p.resolutionTime stays 0, ProposalResolved event never fires.
data_flow_trace.current_state_summary: resolve() remains a permissionless but optional function. If never called, isResolved=false and resolutionTime=0 persist. This precondition for the attack is still reachable.

data_flow_trace.original: Executor calls Governance.execute(id, targets, datas). getProposalState(id) enters the expired-unresolved branch (now_>=expirationTime; yesVotes>noVotes && yes+no>=minVoteThreshold) and returns SUCCEEDED.
data_flow_trace.current_state_summary: execute() now has an EXPLICIT resolution gate BEFORE the state check: lines 505-508 `if (!_proposals[proposalId].isResolved) { revert Errors.ProposalNotResolved(proposalId); }`. Only AFTER this check does it compute getProposalState(). The expired-unresolved SUCCEEDED branch at getProposalState lines 171-174 still exists but is unreachable from execute() because the !isResolved revert fires first.
data_flow_trace.gating_conditions: execute() requires: onlyExecutor (line 486), arrays validated (488-493), proposal exists (496-498), not already executed (501-503), ISRESOLVED TRUE (506-508), state==SUCCEEDED (511-514), hash matches (517-521).
data_flow_trace.differs_from_original: CRITICAL DIFFERENCE: a new `if (!_proposals[proposalId].isResolved) revert ProposalNotResolved(...)` guard was added between the 'already executed' check and the 'succeeded' check. Introduced by commit 7af7ed9 (`fix: Governance and NativeOracle security patches (PR-B) (#58)`), Errors.sol:316 adds the error selector.

data_flow_trace.original: execute() proceeds, sets executed[id]=true, runs the call loop, emits ProposalExecuted. isResolved still false; resolutionTime still 0; ProposalResolved never emitted.
data_flow_trace.current_state_summary: Unreachable under the attack precondition (isResolved=false). The code path that would set executed[id]=true and emit ProposalExecuted is guarded by the new revert at line 507, so the inconsistent state (executed=true while isResolved=false/resolutionTime=0) can no longer occur.
data_flow_trace.gating_conditions: Requires passing step 4's !isResolved check, which fails under the exploit preconditions.
data_flow_trace.differs_from_original: Reachability eliminated — the inconsistent post-state described in the report can no longer be produced through execute().

reasoning_chain: The issue hinges on execute() relying solely on getProposalState()==SUCCEEDED, which can return SUCCEEDED via the expired-unresolved branch (Governance.sol:171-174) without requiring p.isResolved==true. The current code (post-commit 7af7ed9, PR #58) adds a dedicated pre-check at Governance.sol:505-508 — `if (!_proposals[proposalId].isResolved) revert Errors.ProposalNotResolved(proposalId)` — placed BEFORE the state comparison. The new error selector is declared at foundation/Errors.sol:316. Consequently, any attempt to execute a proposal where resolve() was skipped now reverts with ProposalNotResolved, so: (a) executed[id] cannot be flipped to true, (b) the ProposalExecuted event cannot be emitted, (c) the claimed 'executed=true while isResolved=false' state is no longer reachable, (d) off-chain consumers that rely on ProposalResolved as the canonical signal are preserved — every execution is now preceded by a resolve() call which emits ProposalResolved. Commit history (`git log --oneline -- src/governance/Governance.sol`) shows 7af7ed9 with message 'Enforced check in ensuring proposals are strictly resolved before execution', matching the exact mitigation expected by the report.

key_evidence.why_matters: This is the exact mitigation that blocks the expired-unresolved SUCCEEDED bypass. Placed before the getProposalState() check at line 511, it guarantees execute() can only proceed on a proposal whose isResolved field is true, i.e., one that went through resolve() and thereby emitted ProposalResolved and wrote resolutionTime.
Confirms the new error selector underpinning the guard — indicates this was a deliberate, dedicated fix rather than an incidental side-effect.

Demonstrates that getProposalState() STILL has the expired-unresolved SUCCEEDED branch — so the bypass is not removed at the state-machine level; it is blocked by a dedicated guard in execute(). This is important for evaluating whether the fix is complete: other call-sites of getProposalState() could in principle still observe SUCCEEDED for unresolved proposals, but only execute() mutates state.

key_evidence.excerpt: function resolve(uint64 proposalId) external { ... p.isResolved = true; p.resolutionTime = now_; ... emit ProposalResolved(proposalId, state); }
key_evidence.why_matters: resolve() is the only writer of isResolved=true and the only emitter of ProposalResolved. Because execute() now requires isResolved==true, it is the only path to reach execution — guaranteeing ProposalResolved is emitted and resolutionTime!=0 prior to ProposalExecuted, restoring the invariant claimed in the issue impact.

counterargument: One could argue the bug still partially exists because getProposalState() (Governance.sol:171-174) itself still reports SUCCEEDED for expired-unresolved proposals — so external contracts or off-chain consumers that call getProposalState() directly may still receive SUCCEEDED before resolve() runs, which could cause stale/premature UI signals. However, the issue's stated impact is specifically tied to execute() mutating the proposal to EXECUTED without resolve() having fired, producing the contradictory (executed=true, isResolved=false, resolutionTime=0) state and skipping ProposalResolved. That mutation path is now closed at Governance.sol:505-508, so the concrete state-corruption and event-ordering consequences described in the report cannot materialize. The residual view-layer quirk of getProposalState() reporting SUCCEEDED before resolve() is a distinct, arguably intentional preview behavior (it tells callers the proposal will succeed if resolve() is called) and is not the vulnerability described here.
related_existing_tests: test/unit/governance/Governance.t.sol::test_RevertWhen_ExecuteProposalNotSucceeded (calls resolve() before execute(), so it doesn't exercise the new !isResolved guard)
test/unit/governance/Governance.t.sol::test_Execute (happy path; goes through resolve() then execute())
No dedicated regression test was added in commit 7af7ed9 for the ProposalNotResolved revert path — a gap worth flagging, though the fix itself is straightforward.
